      84              : 
      85              : /*---------------------------------------------------------------------------
      86              : 
      87              :         Adding system caches:
      88              : 
      89              :         Add your new cache to the list in include/utils/syscache.h.
      90              :         Keep the list sorted alphabetically.
      91              : 
      92              :         Add your entry to the cacheinfo[] array below. All cache lists are
      93              :         alphabetical, so add it in the proper place.  Specify the relation OID,
      94              :         index OID, number of keys, key attribute numbers, and initial number of
      95              :         hash buckets.
      96              : 
      97              :         The number of hash buckets must be a power of 2.  It's reasonable to
      98              :         set this to the number of entries that might be in the particular cache
      99              :         in a medium-size database.
     100              : 
     101              :         There must be a unique index underlying each syscache (ie, an index
     102              :         whose key is the same as that of the cache).  If there is not one
     103              :         already, add definitions for it to include/catalog/indexing.h: you need
     104              :         to add a DECLARE_UNIQUE_INDEX macro and a #define for the index OID.
     105              :         (Adding an index requires a catversion.h update, while simply
     106              :         adding/deleting caches only requires a recompile.)
     107              : 
     108              :         Finally, any place your relation gets heap_insert() or
     109              :         heap_update() calls, use CatalogTupleInsert() or CatalogTupleUpdate()
     110              :         instead, which also update indexes.  The heap_* calls do not do that.
     111              : 
     112              : *---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     113              : */
     114              : 
     115              : /*
     116              :  *              struct cachedesc: information defining a single syscache
     117              :  */
     118              : struct cachedesc
     119              : {
     120              :         Oid                     reloid;                 /* OID of the relation being cached */
     121              :         Oid                     indoid;                 /* OID of index relation for this cache */
     122              :         int                     nkeys;                  /* # of keys needed for cache lookup */
     123              :         int                     key[4];                 /* attribute numbers of key attrs */
     124              :         int                     nbuckets;               /* number of hash buckets for this cache */
     125              : };
     126              :

     988              : 
     989              : static CatCache *SysCache[SysCacheSize];
     990              : 
     991              : static bool CacheInitialized = false;
     992              : 
     993              : /* Sorted array of OIDs of tables that have caches on them */
     994              : static Oid      SysCacheRelationOid[SysCacheSize];
     995              : static int      SysCacheRelationOidSize;
     996              : 
     997              : /* Sorted array of OIDs of tables and indexes used by caches */
     998              : static Oid      SysCacheSupportingRelOid[SysCacheSize * 2];
     999              : static int      SysCacheSupportingRelOidSize;
    1000              : 
    1001              : static int      oid_compare(const void *a, const void *b);
    1002              : 
    1003              : 
    1004              : /*
    1005              :  * InitCatalogCache - initialize the caches
    1006              :  *
    1007              :  * Note that no database access is done here; we only allocate memory
    1008              :  * and initialize the cache structure.  Interrogation of the database
    1009              :  * to complete initialization of a cache happens upon first use
    1010              :  * of that cache.
    1011              :  */
    1012              : void
    1013            8 : InitCatalogCache(void)
    1014              : {
    1015              :         int                     cacheId;
    1016              : 
    1017              :         StaticAssertStmt(SysCacheSize == (int) lengthof(cacheinfo),
    1018              :                                          "SysCacheSize does not match syscache.c's array");
    1019              : 
    1020              :         Assert(!CacheInitialized);
    1021              : 
    1022            8 :         SysCacheRelationOidSize = SysCacheSupportingRelOidSize = 0;
    1023              : 
    1024          632 :         for (cacheId = 0; cacheId < SysCacheSize; cacheId++)
    1025              :         {
    1026         1248 :                 SysCache[cacheId] = InitCatCache(cacheId,
    1027          624 :                                                                                  cacheinfo[cacheId].reloid,
    1028          624 :                                                                                  cacheinfo[cacheId].indoid,
    1029          624 :                                                                                  cacheinfo[cacheId].nkeys,
    1030          624 :                                                                                  cacheinfo[cacheId].key,
    1031          624 :                                                                                  cacheinfo[cacheId].nbuckets);
    1032          624 :                 if (!PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]))
    1033            0 :                         elog(ERROR, "could not initialize cache %u (%d)",
    1034              :                                  cacheinfo[cacheId].reloid, cacheId);
    1035              :                 /* Accumulate data for OID lists, too */
    1036          624 :                 SysCacheRelationOid[SysCacheRelationOidSize++] =
    1037          624 :                         cacheinfo[cacheId].reloid;
    1038          624 :                 SysCacheSupportingRelOid[SysCacheSupportingRelOidSize++] =
    1039          624 :                         cacheinfo[cacheId].reloid;
    1040          624 :                 SysCacheSupportingRelOid[SysCacheSupportingRelOidSize++] =
    1041          624 :                         cacheinfo[cacheId].indoid;
    1042              :                 /* see comments for RelationInvalidatesSnapshotsOnly */
    1043              :                 Assert(!RelationInvalidatesSnapshotsOnly(cacheinfo[cacheId].reloid));
    1044              :         }
    1045              : 
    1046              :         Assert(SysCacheRelationOidSize <= lengthof(SysCacheRelationOid));
    1047              :         Assert(SysCacheSupportingRelOidSize <= lengthof(SysCacheSupportingRelOid));
    1048              : 
    1049              :         /* Sort and de-dup OID arrays, so we can use binary search. */
    1050            8 :         pg_qsort(SysCacheRelationOid, SysCacheRelationOidSize,
    1051              :                          sizeof(Oid), oid_compare);
    1052            8 :         SysCacheRelationOidSize =
    1053            8 :                 qunique(SysCacheRelationOid, SysCacheRelationOidSize, sizeof(Oid),
    1054              :                                 oid_compare);
    1055              : 
    1056            8 :         pg_qsort(SysCacheSupportingRelOid, SysCacheSupportingRelOidSize,
    1057              :                          sizeof(Oid), oid_compare);
    1058            8 :         SysCacheSupportingRelOidSize =
    1059            8 :                 qunique(SysCacheSupportingRelOid, SysCacheSupportingRelOidSize,
    1060              :                                 sizeof(Oid), oid_compare);
    1061              : 
    1062            8 :         CacheInitialized = true;
    1063            8 : }
    1064              : 
    1065              : /*
    1066              :  * InitCatalogCachePhase2 - finish initializing the caches
    1067              :  *
    1068              :  * Finish initializing all the caches, including necessary database
    1069              :  * access.
    1070              :  *
    1071              :  * This is *not* essential; normally we allow syscaches to be initialized
    1072              :  * on first use.  However, it is useful as a mechanism to preload the
    1073              :  * relcache with entries for the most-commonly-used system catalogs.
    1074              :  * Therefore, we invoke this routine when we need to write a new relcache
    1075              :  * init file.
    1076              :  */
    1077              : void
    1078            3 : InitCatalogCachePhase2(void)
    1079              : {
    1080              :         int                     cacheId;
    1081              : 
    1082              :         Assert(CacheInitialized);
    1083              : 
    1084          237 :         for (cacheId = 0; cacheId < SysCacheSize; cacheId++)
    1085          234 :                 InitCatCachePhase2(SysCache[cacheId], true);
    1086            3 : }
    1087              : 
    1088              : 
    1089              : /*
    1090              :  * SearchSysCache
    1091              :  *
    1092              :  *      A layer on top of SearchCatCache that does the initialization and
    1093              :  *      key-setting for you.
    1094              :  *
    1095              :  *      Returns the cache copy of the tuple if one is found, NULL if not.
    1096              :  *      The tuple is the 'cache' copy and must NOT be modified!
    1097              :  *
    1098              :  *      When the caller is done using the tuple, call ReleaseSysCache()
    1099              :  *      to release the reference count grabbed by SearchSysCache().  If this
    1100              :  *      is not done, the tuple will remain locked in cache until end of
    1101              :  *      transaction, which is tolerable but not desirable.
    1102              :  *
    1103              :  *      CAUTION: The tuple that is returned must NOT be freed by the caller!
    1104              :  */
    1105              : HeapTuple
    1106        12519 : SearchSysCache(int cacheId,
    1107              :                            Datum key1,
    1108              :                            Datum key2,
    1109              :                            Datum key3,
    1110              :                            Datum key4)
    1111              : {
    1112              :         Assert(cacheId >= 0 && cacheId < SysCacheSize &&
    1113              :                    PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]));
    1114              : 
    1115        12519 :         return SearchCatCache(SysCache[cacheId], key1, key2, key3, key4);
    1116              : }
    1117              : 
    1118              : HeapTuple
    1119        69701 : SearchSysCache1(int cacheId,
    1120              :                                 Datum key1)
    1121              : {
    1122              :         Assert(cacheId >= 0 && cacheId < SysCacheSize &&
    1123              :                    PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]));
    1124              :         Assert(SysCache[cacheId]->cc_nkeys == 1);
    1125              : 
    1126        69701 :         return SearchCatCache1(SysCache[cacheId], key1);
    1127              : }
    1128              : 
    1129              : HeapTuple
    1130         7392 : SearchSysCache2(int cacheId,
    1131              :                                 Datum key1, Datum key2)
    1132              : {
    1133              :         Assert(cacheId >= 0 && cacheId < SysCacheSize &&
    1134              :                    PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]));
    1135              :         Assert(SysCache[cacheId]->cc_nkeys == 2);
    1136              : 
    1137         7392 :         return SearchCatCache2(SysCache[cacheId], key1, key2);
    1138              : }
    1139              : 
    1140              : HeapTuple
    1141         3326 : SearchSysCache3(int cacheId,
    1142              :                                 Datum key1, Datum key2, Datum key3)
    1143              : {
    1144              :         Assert(cacheId >= 0 && cacheId < SysCacheSize &&
    1145              :                    PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]));
    1146              :         Assert(SysCache[cacheId]->cc_nkeys == 3);
    1147              : 
    1148         3326 :         return SearchCatCache3(SysCache[cacheId], key1, key2, key3);
    1149              : }
    1150              : 
    1151              : HeapTuple
    1152         2632 : SearchSysCache4(int cacheId,
    1153              :                                 Datum key1, Datum key2, Datum key3, Datum key4)
    1154              : {
    1155              :         Assert(cacheId >= 0 && cacheId < SysCacheSize &&
    1156              :                    PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]));
    1157              :         Assert(SysCache[cacheId]->cc_nkeys == 4);
    1158              : 
    1159         2632 :         return SearchCatCache4(SysCache[cacheId], key1, key2, key3, key4);
    1160              : }
    1161              : 
    1162              : /*
    1163              :  * ReleaseSysCache
    1164              :  *              Release previously grabbed reference count on a tuple
    1165              :  */
    1166              : void
    1167        83467 : ReleaseSysCache(HeapTuple tuple)
    1168              : {
    1169        83467 :         ReleaseCatCache(tuple);
    1170        83467 : }
    1171              : 
    1172              : /*
    1173              :  * SearchSysCacheLocked1
    1174              :  *
    1175              :  * Combine SearchSysCache1() with acquiring a LOCKTAG_TUPLE at mode
    1176              :  * InplaceUpdateTupleLock.  This is a tool for complying with the
    1177              :  * README.tuplock section "Locking to write inplace-updated tables".  After
    1178              :  * the caller's heap_update(), it should UnlockTuple(InplaceUpdateTupleLock)
    1179              :  * and ReleaseSysCache().
    1180              :  *
    1181              :  * The returned tuple may be the subject of an uncommitted update, so this
    1182              :  * doesn't prevent the "tuple concurrently updated" error.
    1183              :  */
    1184              : HeapTuple
    1185           96 : SearchSysCacheLocked1(int cacheId,
    1186              :                                           Datum key1)
    1187              : {
    1188           96 :         CatCache   *cache = SysCache[cacheId];
    1189              :         ItemPointerData tid;
    1190              :         LOCKTAG         tag;
    1191              : 
    1192              :         /*----------
    1193              :          * Since inplace updates may happen just before our LockTuple(), we must
    1194              :          * return content acquired after LockTuple() of the TID we return.  If we
    1195              :          * just fetched twice instead of looping, the following sequence would
    1196              :          * defeat our locking:
    1197              :          *
    1198              :          * GRANT:   SearchSysCache1() = TID (1,5)
    1199              :          * GRANT:   LockTuple(pg_class, (1,5))
    1200              :          * [no more inplace update of (1,5) until we release the lock]
    1201              :          * CLUSTER: SearchSysCache1() = TID (1,5)
    1202              :          * CLUSTER: heap_update() = TID (1,8)
    1203              :          * CLUSTER: COMMIT
    1204              :          * GRANT:   SearchSysCache1() = TID (1,8)
    1205              :          * GRANT:   return (1,8) from SearchSysCacheLocked1()
    1206              :          * VACUUM:  SearchSysCache1() = TID (1,8)
    1207              :          * VACUUM:  LockTuple(pg_class, (1,8))  # two TIDs now locked for one rel
    1208              :          * VACUUM:  inplace update
    1209              :          * GRANT:   heap_update() = (1,9)  # lose inplace update
    1210              :          *
    1211              :          * In the happy case, this takes two fetches, one to determine the TID to
    1212              :          * lock and another to get the content and confirm the TID didn't change.
    1213              :          *
    1214              :          * This is valid even if the row gets updated to a new TID, the old TID
    1215              :          * becomes LP_UNUSED, and the row gets updated back to its old TID.  We'd
    1216              :          * still hold the right LOCKTAG_TUPLE and a copy of the row captured after
    1217              :          * the LOCKTAG_TUPLE.
    1218              :          */
    1219           96 :         ItemPointerSetInvalid(&tid);
    1220              :         for (;;)
    1221           96 :         {
    1222              :                 HeapTuple       tuple;
    1223          192 :                 LOCKMODE        lockmode = InplaceUpdateTupleLock;
    1224              : 
    1225          192 :                 tuple = SearchSysCache1(cacheId, key1);
    1226          192 :                 if (ItemPointerIsValid(&tid))
    1227              :                 {
    1228           96 :                         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
    1229              :                         {
    1230            0 :                                 LockRelease(&tag, lockmode, false);
    1231            0 :                                 return tuple;
    1232              :                         }
    1233           96 :                         if (ItemPointerEquals(&tid, &tuple->t_self))
    1234           96 :                                 return tuple;
    1235            0 :                         LockRelease(&tag, lockmode, false);
    1236              :                 }
    1237           96 :                 else if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
    1238            0 :                         return tuple;
    1239              : 
    1240           96 :                 tid = tuple->t_self;
    1241           96 :                 ReleaseSysCache(tuple);

    1479              : /*
    1480              :  * SysCacheGetAttr
    1481              :  *
    1482              :  *              Given a tuple previously fetched by SearchSysCache(),
    1483              :  *              extract a specific attribute.
    1484              :  *

    1486              :  * from a non-cached relation.  Usually, this is only used for attributes
    1487              :  * that could be NULL or variable length; the fixed-size attributes in
    1488              :  * a system table are accessed just by mapping the tuple onto the C struct
    1489              :  * declarations from include/catalog/.
    1490              :  *
    1491              :  * As with heap_getattr(), if the attribute is of a pass-by-reference type
    1492              :  * then a pointer into the tuple data area is returned --- the caller must
    1493              :  * not modify or pfree the datum!
    1494              :  *
    1495              :  * Note: it is legal to use SysCacheGetAttr() with a cacheId referencing
    1496              :  * a different cache for the same catalog the tuple was fetched from.
    1497              :  */

    1505              :          * can apply heap_getattr().  Normally the cache control data is already
    1506              :          * valid (because the caller recently fetched the tuple via this same
    1507              :          * cache), but there are cases where we have to initialize the cache here.
    1508              :          */
    1509         3368 :         if (cacheId < 0 || cacheId >= SysCacheSize ||
    1510         3368 :                 !PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]))
    1511            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "invalid cache ID: %d", cacheId);
    1512         3368 :         if (!PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]->cc_tupdesc))
    1513              :         {
    1514            5 :                 InitCatCachePhase2(SysCache[cacheId], false);
    1515              :                 Assert(PointerIsValid(SysCache[cacheId]->cc_tupdesc));
    1516              :         }
    1517              : 
    1518         3368 :         return heap_getattr(tup, attributeNumber,
    1519              :                                                 SysCache[cacheId]->cc_tupdesc,
    1520              :                                                 isNull);
    1521              : }

    1583              : /*
    1584              :  * Certain relations that do not have system caches send snapshot invalidation
    1585              :  * messages in lieu of catcache messages.  This is for the benefit of
    1586              :  * GetCatalogSnapshot(), which can then reuse its existing MVCC snapshot
    1587              :  * for scanning one of those catalogs, rather than taking a new one, if no
    1588              :  * invalidation has been received.
    1589              :  *
    1590              :  * Relations that have syscaches need not (and must not) be listed here.  The
    1591              :  * catcache invalidation messages will also flush the snapshot.  If you add a
    1592              :  * syscache for one of these relations, remove it from this list.
    1593              :  */

    1606        31268 :                         return true;
    1607        31122 :                 default:
    1608        31122 :                         break;
    1609              :         }
    1610              : 
    1611        31122 :         return false;
    1612              : }
